tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: The release SHALL generate a canonical audit manifest for every repository, toolchain, Git dependency, dataset, fixture, evidence input, normative document, and external source used for qualification, and SHALL NOT accept mutable or abbreviated repository identities.
tasks.residual_risk: Remote heads can advance after the dated snapshot; only recorded commits qualify. GitHub-only storage is not an independent archive.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: The v1 audit schema rejects missing and unexpected keys and records the API inventories.
review.correctness_and_invariants: The baseline commit/tree, 120-task chain, inputs and artifact hashes are validated exactly.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: Canonical JSON is order-independent and idempotent; toolchains and source revisions are frozen.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: The release README documents deterministic generation and verification commands.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: Selected, reciprocal and unclaimed ecosystem repositories are separated by qualification state.
review.observability_and_provenance: Every local audit artifact has path, purpose, size and SHA-256 plus repository/tool identities.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: Hashing is streamed in fixed chunks over a finite reviewed inventory.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: Missing, malformed or mutable identities fail the audit instead of degrading to an inferred pass.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: Duplicate keys, substituted DOI metadata and abbreviated/oversized revisions have negative tests.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: Positive integration, malformed, boundary, adversarial and metamorphic auditor tests pass.

tasks.title: Create an immutable audit manifest for every repository, toolchain, dataset and external paper used.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: Every 0.9.0 public claim SHALL be classified as IMPLEMENTED, VALIDATED, DEPLOYMENT_QUALIFIED, or NOT_CLAIMED.
Component or synthetic evidence SHALL NOT be represented as deployment qualification, and unsupported behavior SHALL remain NOT_CLAIMED with a concrete limitation.
tasks.residual_risk: Claim evidence is maintainer-generated component evidence; it does not establish field or independent validation.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: The verifier rejects unknown tiers, duplicate IDs, missing fields and affirmative evidence on NOT_CLAIMED entries.
review.correctness_and_invariants: Eleven scoped claims use exactly one frozen tier and an explicit limitation.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: The canonical claims object is embedded in the generated audit manifest.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: The human claims page explains tiers without expanding the machine contract.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: NCP 1.0, upstream pid-rs release and downstream consumers remain explicitly unclaimed.
review.observability_and_provenance: Affirmative claim evidence paths are required to exist and are retained with the release.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: Claims introduce no runtime work or unbounded state.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: No deployment-qualified claim exists and all unsafe inference boundaries are explicit non-claims.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: Truth, intent, policy and secured-deployment claims are denied rather than inferred.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: The auditor proves all claims are tiered/limited and deployment qualification remains empty.

tasks.title: Write a normative 1.0 claims matrix separating implemented, validated, deployment-qualified and explicitly unclaimed behavior.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: Every stable and optional report scalar and verdict SHALL have the exact estimand, ordered decision functional, assumptions and scope documented in the statistical contract.
A report SHALL NOT be interpreted as sensor truth, malicious intent, causal attack class, calibrated posterior, or unlimited-stream error guarantee.
tasks.residual_risk: Repeated-look calibration, missingness identification and field-population validity are not established and remain later blockers/non-claims.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: The auditor checks representative fields from every report family and both fused/PID verdict vocabularies.
review.correctness_and_invariants: NIS, CUSUM, correlation, fusion and optional PID fields map to the implementation's exact equations and ordered branches.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: Inputs, configuration and deterministic decision order fully identify the report functional.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: Equations, thresholds, branch precedence and assumption boundaries are in one normative document.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: Producer/transport authenticity and downstream policy remain outside the statistical claim.
review.observability_and_provenance: Identity fields and non-normative note fields are separated from statistical estimands.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: The contract records retained windows and estimator work as bounded inputs, not implicit populations.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: Insufficiency, unclassified evidence and malformed input remain distinct from nominal evidence.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: The contract names censoring, coordinated statistics-preserving evasion and provenance limitations.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: Core/PID unit, property and workspace tests exercise the documented scalar and verdict branches.

tasks.title: Define the exact statistical estimands for every report field and verdict.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: Invalid, stale, missing, replayed, contradictory, incomparable or resource-excess evidence SHALL NOT become Nominal.
The threat model SHALL cover spoofing, correlated compromise, missingness, timing faults, route confusion and denial of service with required behavior and residual risk.
tasks.residual_risk: Statistics-preserving coordinated attacks and privileged producer/config compromise remain capable of evasion; deployment controls are independently required.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: The model distinguishes application decode limits from pre-materialization transport limits.
review.correctness_and_invariants: Assets, trust boundaries, adversaries and safe-failure states are explicitly enumerated.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: Each threat maps to deterministic required behavior and a visible residual non-claim.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: A compact threat table provides required response and residual risk per class.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: External deployment and consumer responsibilities are not laundered into component claims.
review.observability_and_provenance: Health/fault evidence and authenticated versus merely claimed provenance are separated.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: Memory, payload, track, queue, replay and estimator-work exhaustion are explicit threat classes.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: Malformed inputs error, unidentifiable evidence abstains, and transport integrity faults stop the epoch.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: Single/colluding spoof, replay, route confusion, malformed data, overload, substitution and policy misuse are covered.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: The auditor verifies all six handoff threat classes; the workspace exercises detailed negative cases.

tasks.title: Write a threat model covering spoofing, correlated compromise, missingness, timing faults, route confusion and denial of service.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: Galadriel output SHALL remain advisory and SHALL NOT grant authority, widen a limit, extend TTL or lease, refresh a watchdog, add capabilities, or erase an independent fault.
Record-only handling SHALL preserve the entire policy snapshot and restrict-only handling SHALL be monotonically non-widening.
tasks.residual_risk: The model cannot force an external consumer to call it or prove that its capability digest covers an external policy; no consumer is qualified.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: Private fields and getters prevent unvalidated alternate representations; types are in the stable core inventory.
review.correctness_and_invariants: A pure fixed-integer snapshot comparator implements exact equality and partial-order checks.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: The validator has no clock, IO, randomness, floating point or mutable global state.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: The API explains consumer-selected units and verdict-independent behavior, including Nominal.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: No downstream adapter or control credential is introduced; consumer qualification remains separate.
review.observability_and_provenance: Typed AuthorityViolation errors retain the exact rejected invariant without applying policy.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: Validation is constant-time over one fixed-size snapshot and 32-byte digest.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: Record-only rejects even restrictions; restrict-only admits Allow-to-Deny and non-increasing limits only.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: Deny-to-Allow, each scalar increase, capability substitution and watchdog refresh are negative-tested.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: Positive, negative, equality/zero/MAX boundary, getter round-trip, adversarial and proptest cases pass; all 30 viable changed-line mutants are caught.

tasks.title: Define advisory-only authority invariants and machine-testable non-widening rules.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: Only galadriel-core SHALL be a stable 0.9.x source API; supporting crates, features and wire adapters SHALL remain experimental.
Numerical implementation helpers SHALL NOT be public, and the pure default graph SHALL NOT enable optional PID or NCP features.
tasks.residual_risk: All code phases may still intentionally add stable core items before final 0.9.0 freeze; a final API snapshot/diff is required.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: The accidental chi2 module is removed and intentional authority surface is shown in the retained diff.
review.correctness_and_invariants: The stable crate and all experimental supporting surfaces are named without ambiguity.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: Before/after public API snapshots are generated with pinned cargo-public-api 0.52.0.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: Compatibility scope, breaking-change rule and non-crates.io status are public.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: pid-rs/NCP adapters are not promoted into the stable source contract.
review.observability_and_provenance: API snapshots, tool identity and exact baseline commit are part of the audit record.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: The no-default core build proves the selected stable surface retains its light graph.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: Stable API status does not imply scientific or deployment qualification.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: Optional heavy/network features stay outside the default graph and cannot silently expand attack surface.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: The auditor rejects a final snapshot exposing chi2; clippy/tests/docs/no-default and full gates pass.

tasks.title: Choose the stable crate and feature surface; remove accidental public APIs.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: Release metadata SHALL identify version 0.9.0 and author Sepehr Mahmoudian and SHALL NOT claim a project DOI, Zenodo record, crates.io publication or production support.
Security reports SHALL use a private advisory channel or the named maintainer contact and SHALL NOT be filed publicly while undisclosed.
tasks.residual_risk: The GitHub source release has no independent preservation identifier; the author will add DOI/Zenodo metadata later.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: CFF 1.2 and SemVer 0.9.0 are used without inventing an identifier.
review.correctness_and_invariants: Cargo, CFF, license and release records agree on author, version and dual license.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: The auditor machine-checks version, author, publish=false and absence of project DOI/Zenodo metadata.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: Support and security routes are separated and response expectations are plain.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: Assistants are prohibited as co-authors and no ecosystem project is assigned release ownership.
review.observability_and_provenance: Citation requests exact version and Git commit; author ownership is unambiguous.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: Metadata changes introduce no runtime cost.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: Support limits and absence of production/archival guarantees are explicit.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: A private vulnerability channel, exact contact and public-disclosure prohibition are documented.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: Negative DOI substitution and full metadata integration checks pass.

tasks.title: Resolve licensing, citation, authorship, support and security-contact metadata.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: Every qualified Git dependency SHALL name and resolve a full immutable revision; qualification commands SHALL use the checksummed Cargo.lock and pinned Rust toolchain.
An upstream version declaration without a release artifact SHALL NOT be represented as a released integration.
tasks.residual_risk: cargo deny reports yanked/duplicate transitive warnings from the pinned Zenoh graph; later supply-chain tasks must disposition them.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: NCP scope is v0.8.0 and pid-rs is an immutable component input, not an inferred release promise.
review.correctness_and_invariants: Cargo.lock resolves pid-rs and NCP packages to the declared full revisions.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: Rust 1.89.0, Cargo.lock checksums and exact Git commits identify the qualification graph.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: The distinction between manifest ranges, lock identity and upstream release status is documented.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: No sibling checkout or cross-repo mutation was used to qualify the pins.
review.observability_and_provenance: The generated manifest lists each Git package/version/source/commit.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: Default features remain light; optional dependency work stays opt-in.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: Unreleased upstream status narrows claims instead of silently accepting a floating version label.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: Unknown sources/wildcards are denied and mutable/invalid revisions are rejected.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: Abbreviated/oversized revision tests, locked full gates and cargo-deny source checks pass.

tasks.title: Replace mutable-version dependencies with immutable release or revision pins for qualification.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: The ledger SHALL contain exactly T000 through T119 in dependency order and SHALL NOT mark a task COMPLETE without SHALL-language, tests, machine evidence, all ten review lenses and residual risk.
tasks.residual_risk: The ledger proves documented review/evidence presence, not the truth of external deployment claims; those remain independently audited.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: Source, disposition and generated-ledger schemas have closed required key sets.
review.correctness_and_invariants: All 120 source tasks and their strict predecessor dependencies are losslessly indexed.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: Source YAML hash and canonical generated ledger prevent unnoticed handoff/task drift.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: The release README explains the source/override/generated separation and verify command.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: Strict order prevents later ecosystem/publication claims from bypassing core qualification.
review.observability_and_provenance: Every closure exposes requirement IDs, exact files/logs, reviews and residual risk.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: The fixed 120-entry pass is bounded and deterministic.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: OPEN remains the default and unsupported closure must be explicit NOT_CLAIMED with reason.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: Duplicate IDs/keys, skipped dependencies, unknown status and evidence-free completion are rejected.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: A direct adversarial test proves prose-only completion fails; chain/count integration tests pass.

tasks.title: Create a requirement-to-evidence ledger with no prose-only closure.

tasks.phase_title: Freeze claims, estimands, threat model and release identity
requirements: Protected main SHALL be the sole 0.9.0 candidate line under the release owner's instruction, and every freeze change SHALL identify affected requirements, tests, evidence, claims and risks.
Commits SHALL be professionally messaged, signed and authored by Sepehr Mahmoudian; cross-repository edits SHALL NOT exceed claimed integration scope or overwrite concurrent work.
tasks.residual_risk: Branch protection may require PR-mediated updates even though main is the candidate; remote protected checks remain authoritative after push.

review.api_schema_and_compatibility: Stable API/schema changes require explicit compatibility disposition during freeze.
review.correctness_and_invariants: The owner-requested main candidate, freeze start/end and candidate/publication gates are explicit.
review.determinism_and_reproducibility: A candidate requires clean tree, immutable graph, verified ledger and retained full commands.
review.documentation_and_operator_usability: Candidate, publication, emergency, old-tag and cross-repo rules are in one root policy.
review.ecosystem_composition_and_governance: Cross-repo authority is narrow and concurrent agent/user work must be preserved.
review.observability_and_provenance: Every exception/change must name requirements, tests, evidence, claims and residual risk.
review.performance_and_bounded_resources: Change control adds review gates without changing runtime resources.
review.safety_and_failure_behavior: Only release-blocking exceptions enter freeze and every substantive change invalidates affected evidence.
review.security_and_adversarial_behavior: Signed commits, protected checks, credential-scope limits and non-erasing withdrawal records are required.
review.testing_and_independent_evidence: The complete phase/full gates are retained before freeze declaration; later changes must rerun them.

tasks.title: Declare the 1.0 release candidate branch, freeze window and change-control rules.
